items.text:
Just in case anyone needs it. I built a Chrome extension for researchers: ArXiv PDF Downloader.

It batch-downloads papers from open tabs (arxiv, huggingface papers, ar5iv), names files by paper title, embeds PDF metadata (title/authors/abstract/categories), and auto-tags topics like NLP/CV/ML/Agents.

No backend. No Python/Node runtime. Just a clean extension workflow.

Repo: 
https://
github.com/prdeepakbabu/a
rxiv-downloader-plugin
…

𝗧𝗲𝘀𝘁-𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 has become one of the most under-discussed but consequential shifts in AI systems. In early 2025, a “long-running” autonomous agent typically meant something that could operate for 45 seconds to a minute before it needed human steering. By late 2025, that stretched to several minutes, often around ten, before intervention was required. As we move through early 2026, that runtime continues to grow, and not linearly. With the rise of multi-agent systems, we are seeing an exponential expansion in how long agents can meaningfully operate on complex tasks. Much like Moore’s Law described the doubling of transistor density over time, we are now observing a similar compounding curve, not in hardware density, but in sustained autonomous runtime and task depth.

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗺𝗮𝗱𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗽𝗼𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗯𝗹𝗲? 
One of the primary constraints was context window limitation. Even when 128K tokens felt large, long-horizon reasoning and execution would quickly saturate memory. That constraint is now being addressed from both directions. On the model side, more efficient attention mechanisms and architectural improvements have expanded effective context windows from early 128K ranges to 1M and even 10M-token equivalents in structured settings. On the systems side, scaffolding has evolved dramatically. Modern multi-agent architectures intelligently decompose problems into sub-parts, spin off specialized sub-agents, and allow each to operate within its own context window. Long-term memory, context compaction, and state tracking are handled at the orchestration layer. Instead of relying on a single model to remember everything, we design systems that distribute cognition. The result is something close to virtually unlimited working context, achieved horizontally rather than by scaling a single model indefinitely.

𝗪𝗵𝗲𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗼𝗲𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗹𝗲𝗮𝗱? 
We are entering a phase of agent teams, systems where entire organizational functions can be orchestrated through coordinated agents. Product design, research, development, infrastructure management, testing, and even marketing can be executed by agent swarms operating more like a collaborative society than a single tool. This is not a binary transition. It will unfold gradually. But the implication is timeline compression. A three-year roadmap becoming a six-month execution cycle is increasingly plausible. That compression enables companies to take bolder bets, entering adjacent markets, testing ambitious products, and experimenting at lower marginal cost. Short-term layoffs are often interpreted as AI replacing jobs, but in many cases they may signal organizations reacting tactically instead of structurally, cutting costs rather than redesigning around an AI-first operating model. The deeper shift is not workforce reduction. It is capability expansion. And we are still early in understanding its full implications.
It is an exciting time to be building in AI

The most important question in agentic AI is not how intelligent a component is.
It’s who decides what happens next.

Tools execute.
Skills encode procedure.
Sub-agents reason under contract.
Agents own the loop.

Control, not intelligence, is the real axis.
New paper + Medium write-up below.
blog - 
https://
medium.com/@prdeepak.babu
/control-not-intelligence-the-missing-axis-in-agentic-ai-choosing-between-tool-subagents-and-707c78aef7bb
…

paper - 
https://

articles/1385959-agent-sub-agent-skill-or-tool-a-practitioner-s-guide-to-extending-agentic-ai-systems
…

Its a beauty to see how coding agents just dont give up on the task it is being asked to solve. Agent comes back with approach 1 > feel risky > I skip perm > comes with approach 2 > still risky > I skip perm > comes up with approach 3 > ... on and on.. until I finally decided to give up. I feel models have got so much better looking at the explorations it is doing, it is us humans who need to adapt to this new intelligence now. #claudecode #codingagents

This is literally the advise I give to people who worry about AI - a significant indication of success in new AI world is "intent to learn AI the modern way to how your learn/work and continually adapt"

It is a strange time to hire. 

I'm seeing this play out across the SPC community in ways that surprise me.

We're in the middle of the biggest shift in how knowledge work gets done — maybe ever. And it's made me realize that the old playbook of filtering for pedigree and years

PhD Openings at IISc Bengaluru | Generative AI & Representation Learning


Hello All,

Our lab is expanding! After 5 years of establishing a strong foundation in Generative AI research, I am excited to announce that I am opening positions for research degrees (PhD and MTech Research) under my supervision at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) for the upcoming cycle.

We are looking for motivated students to work on cutting-edge problems in Representation Learning for Generative Models.

-------------------------------- Why join our lab?-----------------------------

Career Trajectory: A PhD from our group opens doors to top-tier industry research labs and academia globally. Our alumni are currently Scientists in industry research labs, Post-docs at top global universities, and Faculty members at IITs (https://lnkd.in/gqN55jX4) 

Track Record: Our students graduate in 3–5 years with top-tier publications. We publish regularly in A* venues like ICML, ICLR, AAAI, CVPR, and TMLR. [https://lnkd.in/ghCp6nN]

 Funding & Stipends: Most of our students secure top-up stipends/fellowships from sources like PMRF, Kotak-IISc (KIAC), Qualcomm, etc.

Industry Collaboration: We solve real-world problems with giants like Google, Microsoft, IBM, Flipkart, Fujitsu, TCS, AMD, and Qualcomm.

Work with a faculty member who is both an academic and a startup founder (https://lnkd.in/gydsEG2S)

--------------------------- Eligibility & Who Should Apply?--------------------

PhD: Master's degree (with good performance) OR B.Tech with a valid GATE score (Direct PhD).

CFTI Exemption: B.Tech graduates from CFTIs (IITs, NITs, IIITs) with CGPA > 8.0 do not need a GATE score.


 ---------------------------How to Apply (2 Steps)----------------------------

Formal Application: We have started a new center on AI (Kotak-IISc AI-ML Centre). Apply through the official portal here: [https://lnkd.in/gyR5qdwu]


Lab Interest: Please fill out this form so I can track your application specifically for my lab: [https://lnkd.in/ghvqpGtH]

I have 2-3 positions available and look forward to working with you on the next frontiers of AI.

Thank you.

PS: You may write to me directly if you have any questions. 

Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Indian Institute of Science - IISc EECS @ IISc, Bengaluru

Code is no longer the bottleneck.
Clarity is.

In the age of AI, intent becomes the source code — and specs stop being documentation and start being executable.

 Spec-Driven Development
 
https://
medium.com/@prdeepak.babu
/spec-driven-development-when-intent-becomes-the-source-code-3af39f86b9d3
…

#SpecDrivenDevelopment #AgenticAI #SoftwareEngineering #AIEngineering

I think it is time we start adopting "skills". They originated at Anthropic, but now has become an open standard which implies - broader usability across agent platforms including google, openAI and other providers. "Skills" are progressively disclosed unlike "Tools" which are pre-emptively loaded into context windows taking valuable space that otherwise could make longer conversations possible and more accurate because it avoids context rot.

The coolest feature I like about skills is availibility of meta-skill called "skill creator". Not many realize what skill creator unlocks. I see most people think of this as a UX for creating new skill but what many do not get is it can unlock self-learning (buzz-word now and loosely used incl. here) i.e. you can have agent replay logs overnight, understand commonly used workflows and have the skill creator create new skills automatically and optionally reviewed by humans or judged by LLMs.

It is ridiculously simple abstraction for lot of things we care about. After careful observations of patterns it is clear - pareto frontier for agents seems to be file systems, web search/browser, coding as tool or model (LLM) together should field 80% of use-cases. 
https://lnkd.in/gbHHx4GF

time and again these cycles keep repeating. for some reason, we seem to always say "this one is different" when it isn't - part of the problem could be because not many of us haven't lived long enough to experience 2-3 major cycle shifts like the one we are in !

Will AI replace SaaS. One of the tech promoters I admire, his take

One thing I’ve noticed while working with agentic systems is how underutilized file systems still are as a first-class abstraction.
In frontier setups, they’re increasingly doing real work — long-term memory, deep research, coordination — even if we don’t always call it out explicitly.

We already treat infrastructure as code(CDK), diagrams as code (Mermaid), and documentation as code (Markdown). In this post, I connect "file systems" and "code" as the most durable universal abstractions (tools) we’ve used to scale digital work and why AI agents seem to be rediscovering them.

👉 https://lnkd.in/gQpxbkBs

Over the last few weeks I’ve been hacking on personal projects using frontier models, and my perception of current capabilities has taken a major gradient update. Coding, scaffolding, and debugging are compressing, while ideation and orchestration are becoming the dominant loops. It’s forcing me to rethink how I integrate AI into day-to-day work (including non-technical tasks). I am quite convinced, that winning mindset in AI era is rooted in abundance, in understanding how specialists evolve, and in not confusing tasks with purpose.

I captured this shift as a mental model connecting abundance (idea surplus), the evolving role of specialists (depth as multiplier, not gate), and the jobs question (tasks vs. purpose). If you work close to the frontier, these patterns will feel familiar.
https://lnkd.in/g4HzEHEe
